title: एकादश रुद्र स्तोत्रम्
devanagariText:
॥ श्री एकादश रुद्र स्तोत्रम् ॥

॥ ध्यानम् ॥
वन्दे शम्भुमुमापतिं सुरगुरुं वन्दे जगत्कारणं
वन्दे पन्नगभूषणं मृगधरं वन्दे पशूनां पतिम्।
वन्दे सूर्यशशाङ्कवह्निनयनं वन्दे मुकुन्दप्रियं
वन्दे भक्तजनाश्रयं च वरदं वन्दे शिवं शङ्करम्॥

॥ एकादश रुद्राः ॥

॥ १. कपाली ॥
ॐ कपालिने नमः।
कपालमालाभरणं त्रिशूलवरधारिणम्।
ऊर्ध्वरेतसमीशानं कपालिनं नमाम्यहम्॥१॥

॥ २. पिङ्गल ॥
ॐ पिङ्गलाय नमः।
पिङ्गलं जटिलं दिव्यं वृषभध्वजमव्ययम्।
पिङ्गलाक्षमुमाकान्तं पिङ्गलं प्रणमाम्यहम्॥२॥

॥ ३. भीम ॥
ॐ भीमाय नमः।
भीमं भीमपराक्रान्तं भीमरूपं भयङ्करम्।
भीमसेनसमं भीमं भीमं प्रणमाम्यहम्॥३॥

॥ ४. विरूपाक्ष ॥
ॐ विरूपाक्षाय नमः।
विरूपाक्षं महादेवं त्रिनेत्रं विश्वशम्भवम्।
विश्वरूपं विश्वनाथं विरूपाक्षं नमाम्यहम्॥४॥

॥ ५. विलोहित ॥
ॐ विलोहिताय नमः।
विलोहितं महाभागं रक्तवर्णं महाद्युतिम्।
रक्ताम्बरधरं रुद्रं विलोहितं नमाम्यहम्॥५॥

॥ ६. शस्ता ॥
ॐ शस्त्रे नमः।
शस्तारं सर्वभूतानां शासनं शुभलक्षणम्।
शर्वं शाश्वतमीशानं शस्तारं प्रणमाम्यहम्॥६॥

॥ ७. अजपाद ॥
ॐ अजपादाय नमः।
अजपादमनादिं च योगिध्येयमनामयम्।
सर्ववेदान्तसारज्ञमजपादं नमाम्यहम्॥७॥

॥ ८. अहिर्बुध्न्य ॥
ॐ अहिर्बुध्न्याय नमः।
अहिर्बुध्न्यं महातेजं कुण्डलीशक्तिधारिणम्।
सर्पभूषणमीशानमहिर्बुध्न्यं नमाम्यहम्॥८॥

॥ ९. शम्भु ॥
ॐ शम्भवे नमः।
शम्भुं शान्तं जगन्नाथं लोकानुग्रहकारिणम्।
शिवं महेश्वरं स्थाणुं शम्भुं प्रणमाम्यहम्॥९॥

॥ १०. चण्ड ॥
ॐ चण्डाय नमः।
चण्डं चण्डपराक्रान्तं चण्डमुण्डविमर्दिनम्।
चण्डीशं चण्डवेगं च चण्डरुद्रं नमाम्यहम्॥१०॥

॥ ११. भव ॥
ॐ भवाय नमः।
भवं भवानीपतिं च भवभीतिविनाशनम्।
भवार्णवतरं देवं भवरुद्रं नमाम्यहम्॥११॥

॥ फलश्रुति ॥
एकादशानां रुद्राणां स्तोत्रमेतत् सुदुर्लभम्।
यः पठेत् प्रयतो नित्यं सर्वरुद्रप्रसादतः।
सर्वपापविनिर्मुक्तो रुद्रलोकं स गच्छति।
महामृत्युं विजयते दीर्घायुष्यमवाप्नुयात्॥

॥ इति श्री एकादश रुद्र स्तोत्रं सम्पूर्णम् ॥
transliteration:
|| Shri Ekadasha Rudra Stotram ||

|| Dhyanam ||
Vande Shambhum-uma-patim Sura-gurum Vande Jagat-karanam
Vande Pannaga-bhushanam Mriga-dharam Vande Pashunam Patim.
Vande Surya-shashanka-vahni-nayanam Vande Mukunda-priyam
Vande Bhakta-jana-ashrayam Cha Varadam Vande Shivam Shankaram.

|| Ekadasha Rudrah ||

|| 1. Kapali ||
Om Kapaline Namah.
Kapala-mala-bharanam Trishula-varadharinam.
Urdhvaretasam-ishanam Kapalinam Namamy-aham.||1||

|| 2. Pingala ||
Om Pingalaya Namah.
Pingalam Jatilam Divyam Vrishabhadhvajam-avyayam.
Pingalaksham-uma-kantam Pingalam Pranamamy-aham.||2||

|| 3. Bheema ||
Om Bheemaya Namah.
Bheemam Bheema-parakrantam Bheema-rupam Bhayankaram.
Bheemasena-samam Bheemam Bheemam Pranamamy-aham.||3||

|| 4. Virupaksha ||
Om Virupakshaya Namah.
Virupaksham Mahadevam Trinetram Vishva-shambhavam.
Vishvarupam Vishvanatham Virupaksham Namamy-aham.||4||

|| 5. Vilohita ||
Om Vilohitaya Namah.
Vilohitam Mahabhagam Raktavarnam Mahadyutim.
Raktambara-dharam Rudram Vilohitam Namamy-aham.||5||

|| 6. Shasta ||
Om Shastre Namah.
Shastaaram Sarva-bhutanam Shasanam Shubha-lakshanam.
Sharvam Shashvatam-ishanam Shastaaram Pranamamy-aham.||6||

|| 7. Ajapada ||
Om Ajapadaya Namah.
Ajapadam-anadim Cha Yogi-dhyeyam-anamayam.
Sarva-vedanta-sarajnam Ajapadam Namamy-aham.||7||

|| 8. Ahirbudhnya ||
Om Ahirbudhnyaya Namah.
Ahirbudhnyam Mahatejam Kundalishakti-dharinam.
Sarpa-bhushanam-ishanam Ahirbudhnyam Namamy-aham.||8||

|| 9. Shambhu ||
Om Shambhave Namah.
Shambhum Shantam Jagannatham Lokanugraha-karinam.
Shivam Maheshvaram Sthanum Shambhum Pranamamy-aham.||9||

|| 10. Chanda ||
Om Chandaya Namah.
Chandam Chanda-parakrantam Chandamunda-vimardinam.
Chandisham Chandavegam Cha Chanda-rudram Namamy-aham.||10||

|| 11. Bhava ||
Om Bhavaya Namah.
Bhavam Bhavani-patim Cha Bhava-bheeti-vinashanam.
Bhavarnava-taram Devam Bhava-rudram Namamy-aham.||11||

|| Phalashruti ||
Ekadashanam Rudranam Stotram-etat Sudurlabham.
Yah Pathet Prayato Nityam Sarva-rudra-prasadatah.
Sarva-papa-vinirmukto Rudra-lokam Sa Gachchhati.
Maha-mrityum Vijayate Dirgha-ayushyam-avapnuyat.

|| Iti Shri Ekadasha Rudra Stotram Sampoornam ||
hindiMeaning:
ध्यान: शम्भु उमापति, सुरगुरु, जगत्कारण, पन्नगभूषण, मृगधर, पशुपति, सूर्य-चन्द्र-अग्नि नयन, मुकुन्दप्रिय, भक्तजनाश्रय, वरद शिव शंकर को वन्दन।

ग्यारह रुद्र:

१. कपाली — कपालमाला, त्रिशूल, वरधारी, ऊर्ध्वरेता, ईशान कपाली को नमस्कार।

२. पिंगल — पिंगलवर्ण, जटिल, दिव्य, वृषभध्वज, अव्यय, पिंगलाक्ष, उमाकान्त पिंगल को नमस्कार।

३. भीम — भीम, भीमपराक्रान्त, भीमरूप, भयंकर भीम रुद्र को नमस्कार।

४. विरूपाक्ष — महादेव, त्रिनेत्र, विश्वशम्भव, विश्वरूप, विश्वनाथ विरूपाक्ष को नमस्कार।

५. विलोहित — महाभाग, रक्तवर्ण, महाद्युति, रक्ताम्बरधर रुद्र विलोहित को नमस्कार।

६. शस्ता — सर्वभूतों के शासक, शुभलक्षण, शर्व, शाश्वत, ईशान शस्ता को नमस्कार।

७. अजपाद — अनादि, योगिध्येय, अनामय, सर्ववेदान्तसारज्ञ अजपाद को नमस्कार।

८. अहिर्बुध्न्य — महातेज, कुण्डलीशक्तिधारी, सर्पभूषण, ईशान अहिर्बुध्न्य को नमस्कार।

९. शम्भु — शान्त, जगन्नाथ, लोकानुग्रहकारी, शिव, महेश्वर, स्थाणु शम्भु को नमस्कार।

१०. चण्ड — चण्डपराक्रान्त, चण्डमुण्डविमर्दक, चण्डीश, चण्डवेग चण्डरुद्र को नमस्कार।

११. भव — भवानीपति, भवभय-विनाशक, भवार्णवतारक भवरुद्र को नमस्कार।

इस सुदुर्लभ एकादश रुद्र स्तोत्र का नित्य प्रयत्नपूर्वक पाठ करने से सर्वरुद्रप्रसाद होता है, सर्वपापमुक्ति, रुद्रलोक प्राप्ति, महामृत्यु पर विजय और दीर्घायुष्य मिलता है।

viniyog.shloka: अस्य श्रीएकादशरुद्रस्तोत्रमन्त्रस्य वामदेव ऋषिः। अनुष्टुप् छन्दः। एकादश रुद्र देवता। ॐ नमः शिवाय बीजम्। रुद्र शक्तिः। रुद्रलोक प्राप्ति कीलकम्। श्रीएकादश रुद्रप्रीत्यर्थे जपे विनियोगः॥

benefits: Praises all 11 forms of Rudra individually with their unique attributes
Extremely rare (Sudurlabha) — one of the most powerful Shaiva stotras
Invokes victory over maha mrityu (great death) and long life
Purifies from all sins and leads to Rudra Loka
Each Rudra verse is a complete meditation on that aspect
Powerful during Maha Shivaratri, Mondays, and Pradosh Vrat
description: Dedicated to Lord Shiva, the Ekadasha Rudra Stotram (एकादश रुद्र स्तोत्रम्) is a devotional hymn of praise revered in Hindu tradition. This sacred composition is sourced from Shiva Purana / Rudra tradition and continues to inspire devotees worldwide. Regular recitation is believed to grant victory over Maha Mrityu (great death) and long life, praise all 11 forms of Rudra individually with their unique attributes, and purify from all sins and leads to Rudra Loka. This prayer is traditionally recited on Monday (Somvar) and during Maha Shivaratri, Shravan month, and Pradosh to invoke divine grace. Comprising 11 verses, it can be completed in approximately 8 minutes, making it suitable for daily devotional practice. On this page, you can read the complete Ekadasha Rudra Stotram in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.
seoDescription: Read Ekadasha Rudra Stotram — prayers to all 11 forms of Rudra in Sanskrit with Hindi meaning for longevity, protection, and moksha.

seoFaqs.question: Who are the eleven Rudras invoked in the Ekadasha Rudra Stotram?
seoFaqs.answer: The eleven Rudras as listed in the Shiva Purana and the Vayu Purana are: Kapali, Pingala, Bhima, Virupaksha, Vilohita, Shastra (Shasta), Ajapada, Ahirbudhnya, Shambhu, Chanda and Bhava. These are the eleven rudra-aspects born of Brahma's mind to govern the eleven pranas (vital airs) of the human body and the eleven senses (five jnanendriyas, five karmendriyas, and the manas). Each name corresponds to a specific cosmic function: Kapali governs liberation from material attachments, Pingala the solar nadi, Bhima the destruction of fear, Virupaksha unconventional vision, and so on. The Yajurvedic Rudradhyaya treats these as eleven specific addresses to a single Rudra.

seoFaqs.question: What is the relationship between this stotram and the Sri Rudram (Namakam-Chamakam)?
seoFaqs.answer: The Ekadasha Rudra Stotram and Sri Rudram are closely related but distinct liturgical units. Sri Rudram is the Yajurvedic chapter (Taittiriya Samhita 4.5 and 4.7) consisting of the Namakam (anuvakas of namaskaras) and Chamakam (anuvakas of supplications). When the Sri Rudram is chanted eleven times in succession with one Chamakam at the end, it is called Rudri or Rudri-Path, the basic unit of an Ekadasha-Rudra-Abhishekam. The Ekadasha Rudra Stotram, by contrast, is a shorter Sanskrit ashtaka-style hymn that names the eleven Rudras one by one as a meditation aid; it is often recited as a sankalpa before commencing the actual eleven-fold Rudri.

seoFaqs.question: What is the Ekadasha Rudra Abhishekam and how is the stotram used in it?
seoFaqs.answer: The Ekadasha Rudra Abhishekam is a major Shaiva yajna in which eleven priests simultaneously chant the Sri Rudram eleven times each over a Shivalinga (totaling 121 chants per ritual cycle), while abhishekam is performed continuously with milk, curd, ghee, honey, sugar, panchamrita, and concluding Ganga water. The Ekadasha Rudra Stotram is recited at the start of each chant-cycle as a dhyana invocation of one of the eleven Rudras in sequence. A complete Maharudra is eleven Ekadasha Rudras (1331 chants) and an Atirudra is eleven Maharudras (14,641 chants), each scaling up the same liturgy with the same stotram embedded as the dhyana.

seoFaqs.question: Which month or tithi is most auspicious for chanting the Ekadasha Rudra Stotram?
seoFaqs.answer: The Ekadasha Rudra Stotram is most auspiciously chanted on Maha Shivaratri (Phalguna Krishna Chaturdashi), every Pradosham (the 13th tithi twice a month), all Mondays of Shravana, all Mondays of Karthika, and on Rudra Ekadasi which falls on the eleventh tithi of the bright half of any lunar month. Astrologically each of the eleven Rudras is assigned to one calendar month-Kapali to Chaitra, Pingala to Vaishakha, Bhima to Jyeshtha, Virupaksha to Ashada, and so on-so devotees often dedicate one month to chanting only that month's Rudra-name 108 times daily, completing the full eleven-Rudra cycle annually.

seoFaqs.question: What benefits do classical sources attribute to recitation of Ekadasha Rudra Stotram?
seoFaqs.answer: The Shiva Purana's phalashruti for the Ekadasha Rudra invocation lists protection from akala-mrityu (premature death), recovery from chronic illness, removal of grahabadha (planetary affliction, especially Saturn and Rahu), neutralisation of black-magic and curses, success in Vedic studies, victory in just litigation, and ultimately the moksha known as Rudra-sayujya (union with Rudra). Astrologically, the eleven Rudras are propitiated to neutralise eleven specific planetary doshas. Health-wise, the Mrityunjaya effect of the eleven-form Rudra is considered superior to single-form Shiva worship for terminal illness, which is why hospitals near Kashi, Trimbakeshwar and Rameshwaram traditionally arrange Maharudra abhishekams for critically ill patients.

seoFaqs.question: Does the Ekadasha Rudra Stotram require initiation, and who can chant it?
seoFaqs.answer: The Ekadasha Rudra Stotram itself, being a stotra, can be chanted by any devotee without initiation. However, the full Ekadasha Rudra Abhishekam yajna which the stotram introduces is restricted: only properly initiated Brahmin priests with Yajurveda training can chant the Sri Rudram in the formal sankalpa-pradhana ritual, because the Yajurvedic chanting requires precise svara (intonation) and shiksha (phonetic accuracy). For private home recitation, a householder can chant the Ekadasha Rudra Stotram alone, perform a simple panchopachara puja with bilva leaves, and (if Yajurvedic training is lacking) substitute the Rudrashtakam by Tulsidas, which is praised by all acharyas as an equally efficacious alternative.
